title: Make the windowed benchmark advance through the idle-render gate
rationale: The live TzeHouse fullscreen pass rendered exactly one warmup frame, then the production idle-render gate skipped the unchanged composite_tiles_v1 scene until the 30-second watchdog aborted. Because WindowedBenchmarkRunState::record is called only after a presented frame, the harness cannot produce fullscreen/overlay p99 values or evaluate the locked 500 us composite-delta budget.
unblock_condition: A focused fix, with a red-green regression test, makes benchmark mode deterministically present all requested warmup and measured frames without disabling the production idle gate for normal runtime sessions. Rerun only after the companion harness guard below also lands; accept the result only when both mode artifacts report equal effective dimensions on an approved reference surface and windowed_fullscreen_vs_overlay_report.json records composite_delta.p99_us <= 500 or a measured over-budget diagnosis.

title: Make the composite harness reject unequal effective surfaces
rationale: The canonical harness currently forwards explicit --width 1920 --height 1080 to both modes. Production option wiring disables overlay auto-sizing when either dimension is explicit, while fullscreen ignores configured dimensions and uses the monitor. This failed run records fullscreen at 3840x2160, so an unchanged post-idle-gate rerun would compare fullscreen 3840x2160 against overlay 1920x1080 and could produce a meaningless composite delta.
unblock_condition: Update the harness so fullscreen and overlay use the same approved reference-host surface, validate the two emitted window.width/window.height pairs are identical before computing any delta, and add a regression test that rejects mismatched effective dimensions.
